motivation: Today the agent explores codebases by reading files one at a time — every structural question triggers a cascade of grep→read→grep that burns tens of thousands of tokens. A persistent call graph with type-aware resolution answers the same questions in ~200 tokens. The pieces already exist in sprout (tree-sitter symbol extraction, LSP integration, embedding store) but aren't wired into a queryable structural graph.
non_goals: Cypher query engine — our agent queries via structured Go tools, not a query language.
Clone/similarity detection (MinHash/LSH) — niche; falls out of semantic search.
3D graph visualization — could add a WebUI graph view later if there's demand.
158 language grammars — we support Go, TS, JS, Python well; add languages incrementally. Users needing exotic languages can add codebase-memory-mcp via our MCP integration.
summary: Upgrade sprout's flat repo_map into a persistent, incrementally-indexed knowledge graph of symbols and call edges. Gives the agent structural query tools (get_callers, get_callees, find_dead_code) so code navigation becomes a sub-millisecond lookup instead of a grep→read→grep cycle. Leverages existing tree-sitter (pkg/ast), LSP (pkg/lsp), and embedding (pkg/embedding) infrastructure.
title: Code Intelligence Graph — Persistent Call Graph, Dead-Code Detection, and Semantic Code Search
